content: Imagine you are writing a blog post comparing two popular smartphone models. Develop an outline for the blog post, including key points and subheadings to effectively compare and contrast the features, performance, and user experience of the two models. Please answer in fewer than 200 words.

**Outline: “Model A vs. Model B: Which Smartphone Reigns Supreme?”**  

| Section | Key Points | Sub‑Points |
|--------|------------|------------|
| **1. Opening** | Purpose & target audience | Quick hook: “Battle of the best‑selling phones” |
| **2. Design & Build** | Look & feel | Materials, weight, ergonomics, color options |
| **3. Display** | Size, resolution, refresh rate | HDR support, color accuracy, touch latency |
| **4. Camera System** | Megapixels, aperture, software | Low‑light performance, autofocus speed, portrait mode |
| **5. Performance** | CPU/GPU, RAM | Benchmark scores (Geekbench, AnTuTu), real‑world gaming |
| **6. Battery & Charging** | Capacity, endurance tests | Fast‑charge wattage, wireless charging, standby life |
| **7. Software Experience** | OS version, bloatware, customization | UX, updates frequency, ecosystem integration |
| **8. Pricing & Value** | MSRP, variants, 4‑G/5‑G plans | Cost per feature analysis |
| **9. Pros & Cons** | Quick comparison table | Strengths, weaknesses, ideal user profile |
| **10. Verdict** | Recommendation | “Best overall” vs. “Best for photography” vs. “Best budget” |

*Keep each subsection to 2–3 bullets; total word count ≈ 170.*
Take your previous response and rephrase it as a limerick.

When two phones came to the stand,  
Model A and Model B, each in hand,  
We checked display, camera, and core speed,  
Battery, software, all to meet need,  
In verdict, we’ll both take the brand.
